outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Iran: "We Cannot Trust the Americans at All" — Araghchi issues stark warning at BRICS as nuclear deadlock hardens
outlets.summary: Iranian FM Araghchi declares US mistrust the main obstacle to any deal, warning of rising economic costs to Americans from the "war of choice" on Iran.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Israel kills Hamas military chief — the "last architect of October 7" — in Gaza City strike, upending ceasefire prospects
outlets.summary: IDF confirmed the killing of Izz al-Din al-Haddad in a 13-munition strike on Gaza City's Rimal neighborhood, with critics warning the move could collapse the Gaza ceasefire.

outlets.name: MSNBC
outlets.headline: Despite the hype, Trump leaves China empty-handed and no better off than he was before
outlets.summary: Analysis finds Trump-Xi summit produced no binding commitments on Iran despite joint statement language, with US and China "disagreeing on what they agreed on" per Bloomberg.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: IDF eliminates Hamas military chief Haddad, "last architect of Oct. 7"; Netanyahu: "I ordered the killing"
outlets.summary: Fox leads with the confirmed killing of Hamas's top military commander, framing it as a major Israeli-US victory and a key step toward Trump's Gaza reconstruction plan.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump: Would accept 20-year Iran nuclear suspension with real guarantee; warns Iran leaders are "insane" if they test him
outlets.summary: Trump signals policy flexibility on Iran nuclear enrichment duration, while Rubio warns Iranian leaders not to miscalculate US resolve with the ceasefire expiry imminent.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: Ship Seized, Another Sunk Near Strait of Hormuz — Iran Signals It Will Keep Up Maritime Pressure
outlets.summary: Two maritime incidents on May 15 near the Strait of Hormuz underscore continued Iranian pressure despite the ceasefire. An Iranian official reiterated Iran's right to seize US-linked tankers.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war live: Tehran warns more economic woes for US over "war of choice"; Lebanon-Israel truce extended
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera leads with Araghchi's BRICS speech calling out US "bullying" and warning of economic blowback, alongside the Lebanon ceasefire extension and continued IDF strikes in Tyre.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Trump, Xi Project Hormuz Alignment Despite Lack of Iran Progress
outlets.summary: Bloomberg analysis finds the Trump-Xi joint statement on Hormuz glosses over deep strategic divergence — the two sides "disagree on what they agreed on" with no binding Chinese commitment.

outlets.name: PBS NewsHour
outlets.headline: Tensions flare near Strait of Hormuz as one ship is seized and another is sunk
outlets.summary: PBS covers the dual maritime incidents — UKMTO-confirmed seizure near Fujairah and sinking of Indian-flagged Haji Ali off Oman — on Day 75 of the Hormuz blockade.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IDF confirms killing Hamas military chief Haddad; Netanyahu: "last architect of Oct. 7" eliminated
outlets.summary: The Times of Israel leads with the confirmed killing of Izz al-Din al-Haddad, describing the strike as using 13 munitions on the Gaza City building and noting that Netanyahu personally ordered it.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 45 days; IDF strikes Tyre while talks continue in Washington
outlets.summary: The ceasefire extension was announced in Washington after two days of talks, but Israeli strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in Tyre (37 wounded) continued even as the extension was signed.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Netanyahu reveals secret wartime visit to UAE; Abu Dhabi denies it happened
outlets.summary: Netanyahu publicly disclosed a covert visit to the UAE during the Iran war, calling it a "historic breakthrough." The UAE publicly denied the visit, creating an unusual diplomatic contradiction.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Araghchi: Americans are told they must absorb rocketing costs of war of choice on Iran
outlets.summary: PressTV amplifies Araghchi's BRICS speech warning US citizens about the economic toll of the Iran war, framing gas prices, mortgage rates and stock volatility as consequences of US aggression.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian: Iran will never bow before enemies; resistance is not surrender
outlets.summary: Iranian state media broadcasts Pezeshkian's defiance speech, emphasizing that any diplomatic engagement does not signal weakness or retreat from Iran's sovereign positions.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: BRICS ends without joint statement — Iran war exposes deep fractures in the bloc
outlets.summary: Iran International (opposition channel) covers the BRICS collapse, noting that the bloc's inability to reach consensus on the Iran war reflects China's reluctance to confront the US and India's neutrality.
